title: RustinsQuick Dry Poly Varnish Matt Clear 500ml -Tough, Water-Based Clear Finish in Gloss, Matt or Satin; Durable Protection for Interior Wood, Furniture and Floors
features: Extra Tough Quick-Drying Formula – Rustins Quick Dry Polyurethane Varnish combines durability and convenience; dries rapidly to a hard, protective finish that resists scratches, stains and general household wear
Water-Based and Non-Yellowing – Sustainable, low-odour varnish that cleans up easily with water; remains clear without yellowing, preserving the natural colour and grain of interior wood over time
Versatile Interior Protection – Suitable for furniture, doors and skirting boards, available in gloss, matt and satin clear or tinted satin shades for warm wood tones, ADD TO BASKET now
Enhances Natural Wood Beauty – Provides a smooth, even coating that highlights the natural beauty of wood; creates a professional, high-quality finish with lasting clarity and subtle sheen

Trusted Rustins Craftsmanship – Produced by a reliable UK manufacturer known for good-quality and sustainable woodcare products; delivers consistent, long-lasting results trusted by professionals and DIY users alike
description: From the manufacturer Rustins Quick Dry Polyurethane Varnish Range Rustins Quick Dry Polyurethane Varnish A water-based, non-yellowing polyurethane varnish that is tough and durable and will resist cracking, peeling and blistering. Can be used on bare wood, hardboard, MDF, unsealed cork. For interior and exterior use. Long lasting wood protection For all types of interior and exterior wood Low odour & non toxic Enhances the beauty of wood Resists alcohol & boiling water Available in many wood shades ABOUT RUSTINS In 1924 Sidney Rustin opened his first shop in London's Shoreditch district where he sold wood finishing products to local residents and trades people. Sidney's father was a cabinetmaker and his mother funded the business venture. During the second world war the first night raid destroyed the original 1932 factory and the business moved to its present location in Cricklewood, London.
